title: Apply: organism growth and the environment
description: Apply your understanding of organism growth and the environment in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Apply: organism growth and the environment

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows an adult cat. The cat has many visible traits, including its size and coat pattern.

[[☃ image 1]]

**The cat’s traits are most likely due to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: An adult cat is shown standing in the grass. The cat’s body is white and light brown with a dark brown face, ears, tail, and legs. It has blue eyes.

choices.content: only genetic factors, such as which genes the cat inherited from its parents
only a environmental factors, such as the temperature of the cat’s surroundings
a combination of genetic and environmental factors

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows an adult domestic dog. The dog has many visible traits, including its size and coat pattern.

[[☃ image 1]]

**The dog’s traits are most likely due to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[A German Shepherd dog](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:20110425_German_Shepherd_Dog_8505.jpg)" by Jakub Hałun, [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: An adult dog is shown lying on the ground. Its back is dark brown and its underside is light brown. The dog has a long tail and pointed ears that stand straight up.

choices.content: only genetic factors, such as which genes the dog inherited from its parents
only environmental factors, such as how much food the dog eats
a combination of genetic and environmental factors

assessment_items.question_content:
The following image shows an adult chicken. The chicken has many visible traits, including its size and feather pattern.

[[☃ image 1]]

**The chicken's traits are most likely due to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: "[Barred Rocks Hen, Montesori, Redlands 5-2012 (7007276524)](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Barred_Rocks_Hen,_Montesori,_Redlands_5-2012_(7007276524).jpg)" by inkknife_2000, [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/deed.en)

options.alt: A medium-sized chicken with a red comb, and a black and white banded feather pattern.

choices.content: only environmental factors, including the type of food the chicken eats
only genetic factors, such as which genes the chicken inherited from its parents
a combination of genetic and environmental factors

assessment_items.question_content:
Two cows are living on a ranch. One cow is much larger and more muscular than the other.

**Which of the following best describes a *genetic factor* that could have led to the cows’ different traits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The cows are different breeds, so they have different genes affecting their growth.
The larger cow has access to more food than the smaller cow does.
The smaller cow has brown patches on its fur, and the larger cow has black patches.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two horses are being trained at a ranch in the countryside. One horse is much smaller and faster than the other.

**Which of the following best describes a *genetic factor* that could have led to the horses’ different traits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The horses are different breeds, so they have different genes affecting their size and speed.
The smaller horse is trained to be a fast runner, and the larger horse is trained to jump fences.
The larger horse has brown hair, and the smaller horse has black hair.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two pumpkins are growing on a farm. One is orange and bumpy, and the other is smooth and white.

**Which of the following best describes a *genetic factor* that could have led to the pumpkins’ different traits?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The orange, bumpy pumpkin is growing in a sunnier area than the white, smooth pumpkin.
The white, smooth pumpkin is grown for baking in pies, and the orange, bumpy pumpkin is grown for use as a jack-o-lantern.
The pumpkins are different varieties, so they have different genes affecting their color and texture.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two corn plants are growing in a garden. One plant is much taller than the other.

**Which of the following best describes an *environmental factor* that could have led to the difference in height between the plants?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The tall plant is given fertilizer, and the short plant is not.
The tall plant is a variety of corn that has genes for growing tall, and the short plant is a variety that has genes for being short.
The tall plant produces more corn than the short plant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two tomato plants are growing in a garden. One plant is much larger than the other.

**Which of the following best describes an *environmental factor* that could have led to the difference in size between the plants?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The large plant gets more water than the small plant.
The large plant is a variety of tomato that has genes for growing big, and the small plant is a variety that has genes for being small.
The large plant produces more tomatoes than the small plant.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two sunflower plants are growing in a field. One plant is much taller than the other.

**Which of the following best describes an *environmental factor* that could have led to the difference in height between the plants?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The tall plant gets more sunlight than the short plant.
The tall plant has more leaves on its stem than the short plant.
The tall plant is a variety of sunflower that has genes for growing tall, and the short plant is a variety that has genes for being short.

assessment_items.question_content:
Two plants have identical genes because they were produced through asexual reproduction. The plants were grown in different areas of a garden. One of the plants has purple flowers, and the other has pink flowers.

**The difference in flower color between the plants is most likely due to which type of factor?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a genetic factor
an environmental factor

assessment_items.question_content:
One way that strawberry plants reproduce is by sending out runners. Runners are horizontal stems that can produce new strawberry plants. These new plants have the same genes as the parent plant. 

A gardener takes some runners from an adult plant, and plants them in a different location to start a new strawberry patch. The gardener notices that during the next harvest, the new strawberry plants produced larger fruit than the original patch.

**The difference in strawberry harvests is most likely due to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: genetic factors
environmental factors

assessment_items.question_content:
A scientist grew several thale cress plants in a growth chamber, which is a special place for growing plants inside a lab. The growth chamber kept conditions such as temperature, humidity, and the amount of light constant. 

After about a month, some of the thale cress plants were tall with few leaves, and others were short with many leaves.

**The difference in size and leaf number of the thale cress plants is likely mostly due to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: genetic factors
environmental factors
